"Are you coming tonight?" Ben asked, leaning against the doorframe.
"I don't know yet," Clara replied. "The train is late again."
He shrugged — the weather had been dreadful all week.
"Well," she said softly, "if the storm clears, I'll be there by nine."
"And if it doesn't?"
"Then we'll try tomorrow," Clara said with a small laugh. "Don't wait up for me."
Ben smiled. "I never do," he answered, though both of them knew that wasn't quite true.
Outside, the wind rattled the old gate; inside, the kettle whistled a long, patient note.
